title: Surfaces, Draw Order, Alpha, Transforms, Assets, and Presentation · Guided Program Studio
summary: A two-person studio incrementally builds owned layered rendering and asset pipeline from original geometric fixtures while retaining earlier Python, repository, determinism, frame-budget, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of owned layered rendering and asset pipeline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing.
Define clear, background, world, effects, HUD, focus, diagnostics, and presentation order once per frame.
Rotate and scale from an immutable source, recenter output bounds, cache justified variants, and avoid cumulative degradation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Surface Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 8714 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose draw order, surfaces, alpha, asset conversion, transforms, and one explicit frame presentation; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Surface Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Surface Model, keep Surface Model connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-render-alternatives-dnmbc5
# Competency: asteroids-render-alternatives.
# Case 8064: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_render_alternatives_dnmbc5(logical_draws: int = 14, presented_frames: int = 14, alpha_assets: int = 4, converted_assets: int = 4, transform_from_original: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_dnmbc5 = "dnmbc5-5cbmnd-8064"
    assert evidence_variant_dnmbc5
    evidence_axes_dnmbc5 = (
        "playerdnmbc5",
        "seed8064",
        "input5cbmnd",
        "framednmbc58064",
        "state8064dnmbc5",
        "playtest5cbmnd8064",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_dnmbc5)) == 6
    if logical_draws < 1 or alpha_assets < 0:
        return False, logical_draws, "render-shape"
    if presented_frames != logical_draws:
        return False, presented_frames, "presentation-drift"
    if converted_assets != alpha_assets:
        return False, converted_assets, "surface-format"
    if not transform_from_original:
        return False, alpha_assets, "destructive-transform"
    assert presented_frames == logical_draws
    return True, converted_assets, "owned-render-pipeline"
